article_title: Former Rio Tinto CEO's Deep-Sea Mining Firm Merges With Odyssey Marine in $1 Billion Deal to Build US-Controlled Critical Minerals Platform

content_preview.title: Former Rio Tinto CEO's Deep-Sea Mining Firm Merges With Odyssey Marine in $1 Billion Deal to Build US-Controlled Critical Minerals Platform
content_preview.summary: American Ocean Minerals Corporation and Odyssey Marine Exploration announced an all-stock merger valued at approximately $1 billion, creating one of the largest deep-sea mining companies as the US accelerates efforts to secure seabed critical minerals.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

American Ocean Minerals Corporation (AOMC) and Odyssey Marine Exploration (NASDAQ: OMEX) announced on April 8, 2026, that they have entered into a definitive all-stock merger agreement to create what the companies describe as a U.S.-controlled deep-sea critical minerals platform, according to [Mining.com](https://www.mining.com/aomc-odyssey-merge-to-build-1b-deep-sea-miner/). The combined entity, which will trade on Nasdaq under the ticker "AOMC," carries a pro forma equity valuatio...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-sourced business/industry article on deep-sea mining merger with regulatory and environmental context.
editor_notes.source_verification: Mining.com confirms $1B merger, Albanese as chair, all-stock deal, $150M+ financing, Cook Islands/CCZ/Penrhyn Basin exploration, critical minerals focus, Trump executive order context. Claims verified.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified. The 500,000 sq km figure in the article may be an approximation of the Cook Islands EEZ plus CCZ areas.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced, covering both commercial ambitions and environmental concerns.
editor_notes.originality: No prior deep-sea mining merger coverage in the archive.
